rep_1_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_1_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_1_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_2_seed.summary: Your primary care provider has assessed your blood pressure and diabetes management, modifying your prescriptions to improve control.
rep_2_seed.explanation: Managing high blood pressure and diabetes requires regular monitoring of vitals and blood chemistry. This routine visit ensures your medications are functioning properly without side effects, preventing long-term cardiovascular or renal damage.

pathways.title: Prescription Adjustment
pathways.description: Optimizing drug therapy with diet counsel.
pathways.benefits: Maintains BP < 130/80 and HbA1c < 7.0; prevents strokes and kidney damage.
pathways.risks: Potential drug interactions or minor side effects (cough, stomach upset).
pathways.considerations: Essential for active chronic disease control.

questions: Do I need to check my blood sugar at home daily?
Are there generic alternatives to my current prescriptions?
What dietary changes will help reduce my dependency on these medications?
rep_2_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_4_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_4_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_4_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_7_seed.explanation: Routine pediatric checkups occur at key intervals. The pediatrician measures weight, length, head circumference, assesses developmental milestones (sitting, tracking), and administers immunizations to protect against childhood diseases.

rep_8_seed.explanation: Vaginal delivery is the natural birth process. The baby passes through the birth canal. It is the safest method of birth for both mother and baby for uncomplicated pregnancies, with minimal post-birth pain and hospital stay.

rep_11_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_11_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_11_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_14_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_14_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_14_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_24_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_24_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?

rep_32_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_32_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?

rep_38_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_38_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?

rep_41_seed.explanation: Appendicitis is a sudden swelling of the appendix. If not removed quickly, it can burst, spreading bacteria throughout the abdomen and causing life-threatening complications. The surgery removes the appendix via laparoscopy or open incision.

pathways.description: Urgent removal of appendix.

pathways.considerations: Required standard of care for acute appendicitis.

rep_52_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_52_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_52_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_53_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_53_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_53_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_72_seed.explanation: Managing high blood pressure and diabetes requires regular monitoring of vitals and blood chemistry. This routine visit ensures your medications are functioning properly without side effects, preventing long-term cardiovascular or renal damage.

pathways.title: Prescription Adjustment
pathways.description: Optimizing drug therapy with diet counsel.
pathways.benefits: Maintains BP < 130/80 and HbA1c < 7.0; prevents strokes and kidney damage.
pathways.risks: Potential drug interactions or minor side effects (cough, stomach upset).
pathways.considerations: Essential for active chronic disease control.

rep_73_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_73_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?

rep_74_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_74_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_74_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_75_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_75_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_75_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_76_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_76_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_76_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_98_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN has mapped out a vaginal childbirth plan, which is standard for healthy, uncomplicated term pregnancies.
rep_98_seed.explanation: Vaginal delivery is the natural birth process. The baby passes through the birth canal. It is the safest method of birth for both mother and baby for uncomplicated pregnancies, with minimal post-birth pain and hospital stay.

pathways.title: Normal Delivery
pathways.description: Vaginal birth with pain management options.
pathways.benefits: Shortest hospital stay; minimal surgical risks.
pathways.risks: Labor pains; potential minor tears.
pathways.considerations: Recommended for healthy pregnancies with vertex (head-down) baby.

questions: What pain management options (epidural, gas) will be available?
What triggers an emergency C-section during active labor?
How long will I remain in the facility post-birth?
rep_98_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_99_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_99_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_99_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_100_seed.summary: Your pediatrician has scheduled a routine wellness exam to monitor your child's growth, development, and vaccines.
rep_100_seed.explanation: Routine pediatric checkups occur at key intervals. The pediatrician measures weight, length, head circumference, assesses developmental milestones (sitting, tracking), and administers immunizations to protect against childhood diseases.

pathways.title: Routine Baby Visit
pathways.description: Comprehensive wellness check and immunization.
pathways.benefits: Early detection of developmental issues; vaccination defense.
pathways.risks: Mild vaccine fever or soreness.
pathways.considerations: Standard preventative care schedule.

questions: Is my baby meeting all developmental milestones for 6 months?
What side effects should I watch for after today's vaccinations?

rep_101_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_101_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_101_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_102_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_102_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_102_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_103_seed.summary: Your surgical team recommends an emergency appendectomy to remove an inflamed appendix before it ruptures.
rep_103_seed.explanation: Appendicitis is a sudden swelling of the appendix. If not removed quickly, it can burst, spreading bacteria throughout the abdomen and causing life-threatening complications. The surgery removes the appendix via laparoscopy or open incision.

pathways.title: Emergency Appendectomy
pathways.description: Urgent removal of appendix.
pathways.benefits: Prevents rupture; immediate relief from severe infection.
pathways.risks: Infection of incision; brief recovery downtime.
pathways.considerations: Required standard of care for acute appendicitis.

questions: Is the appendix ruptured, or is it intact?
Will this be performed laparoscopically (keyhole) or via open surgery?
How long must I restrict heavy lifting or physical activity?
rep_103_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_109_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_109_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_109_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_124_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_125_seed.summary: Your pediatrician has scheduled a routine wellness exam to monitor your child's growth, development, and vaccines.
rep_125_seed.explanation: Routine pediatric checkups occur at key intervals. The pediatrician measures weight, length, head circumference, assesses developmental milestones (sitting, tracking), and administers immunizations to protect against childhood diseases.

pathways.title: Routine Baby Visit
pathways.description: Comprehensive wellness check and immunization.
pathways.benefits: Early detection of developmental issues; vaccination defense.
pathways.risks: Mild vaccine fever or soreness.
pathways.considerations: Standard preventative care schedule.

questions: Is my baby meeting all developmental milestones for 6 months?
What side effects should I watch for after today's vaccinations?
When is the next scheduled wellness visit?
rep_125_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_126_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_126_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_126_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_128_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN has mapped out a vaginal childbirth plan, which is standard for healthy, uncomplicated term pregnancies.
rep_128_seed.explanation: Vaginal delivery is the natural birth process. The baby passes through the birth canal. It is the safest method of birth for both mother and baby for uncomplicated pregnancies, with minimal post-birth pain and hospital stay.

pathways.title: Normal Delivery
pathways.description: Vaginal birth with pain management options.
pathways.benefits: Shortest hospital stay; minimal surgical risks.
pathways.risks: Labor pains; potential minor tears.
pathways.considerations: Recommended for healthy pregnancies with vertex (head-down) baby.

questions: What pain management options (epidural, gas) will be available?
What triggers an emergency C-section during active labor?
How long will I remain in the facility post-birth?
rep_128_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_129_seed.summary: Your surgical team recommends an emergency appendectomy to remove an inflamed appendix before it ruptures.
rep_129_seed.explanation: Appendicitis is a sudden swelling of the appendix. If not removed quickly, it can burst, spreading bacteria throughout the abdomen and causing life-threatening complications. The surgery removes the appendix via laparoscopy or open incision.

pathways.title: Emergency Appendectomy
pathways.description: Urgent removal of appendix.
pathways.benefits: Prevents rupture; immediate relief from severe infection.
pathways.risks: Infection of incision; brief recovery downtime.
pathways.considerations: Required standard of care for acute appendicitis.

questions: Is the appendix ruptured, or is it intact?
Will this be performed laparoscopically (keyhole) or via open surgery?
How long must I restrict heavy lifting or physical activity?
rep_129_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_134_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_134_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_134_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_140_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_140_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_140_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_143_seed.summary: Your primary care provider has assessed your blood pressure and diabetes management, modifying your prescriptions to improve control.
rep_143_seed.explanation: Managing high blood pressure and diabetes requires regular monitoring of vitals and blood chemistry. This routine visit ensures your medications are functioning properly without side effects, preventing long-term cardiovascular or renal damage.

pathways.title: Prescription Adjustment
pathways.description: Optimizing drug therapy with diet counsel.
pathways.benefits: Maintains BP < 130/80 and HbA1c < 7.0; prevents strokes and kidney damage.
pathways.risks: Potential drug interactions or minor side effects (cough, stomach upset).
pathways.considerations: Essential for active chronic disease control.

questions: Do I need to check my blood sugar at home daily?
Are there generic alternatives to my current prescriptions?
What dietary changes will help reduce my dependency on these medications?

rep_152_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_152_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_152_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_153_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_153_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_153_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_155_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_155_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_155_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_157_seed.explanation: Vaginal delivery is the natural birth process. The baby passes through the birth canal. It is the safest method of birth for both mother and baby for uncomplicated pregnancies, with minimal post-birth pain and hospital stay.

pathways.title: Normal Delivery
pathways.description: Vaginal birth with pain management options.
pathways.benefits: Shortest hospital stay; minimal surgical risks.
pathways.risks: Labor pains; potential minor tears.
pathways.considerations: Recommended for healthy pregnancies with vertex (head-down) baby.

questions: What pain management options (epidural, gas) will be available?

rep_158_seed.summary: Your surgical team recommends an emergency appendectomy to remove an inflamed appendix before it ruptures.
rep_158_seed.explanation: Appendicitis is a sudden swelling of the appendix. If not removed quickly, it can burst, spreading bacteria throughout the abdomen and causing life-threatening complications. The surgery removes the appendix via laparoscopy or open incision.

pathways.title: Emergency Appendectomy
pathways.description: Urgent removal of appendix.
pathways.benefits: Prevents rupture; immediate relief from severe infection.
pathways.risks: Infection of incision; brief recovery downtime.
pathways.considerations: Required standard of care for acute appendicitis.

questions: Is the appendix ruptured, or is it intact?

How long must I restrict heavy lifting or physical activity?

rep_160_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_160_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_160_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_162_seed.summary: Your primary care provider has assessed your blood pressure and diabetes management, modifying your prescriptions to improve control.
rep_162_seed.explanation: Managing high blood pressure and diabetes requires regular monitoring of vitals and blood chemistry. This routine visit ensures your medications are functioning properly without side effects, preventing long-term cardiovascular or renal damage.

pathways.title: Prescription Adjustment
pathways.description: Optimizing drug therapy with diet counsel.
pathways.benefits: Maintains BP < 130/80 and HbA1c < 7.0; prevents strokes and kidney damage.
pathways.risks: Potential drug interactions or minor side effects (cough, stomach upset).
pathways.considerations: Essential for active chronic disease control.

questions: Do I need to check my blood sugar at home daily?
Are there generic alternatives to my current prescriptions?
What dietary changes will help reduce my dependency on these medications?

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_179_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_180_seed.summary: Your pediatrician has scheduled a routine wellness exam to monitor your child's growth, development, and vaccines.
rep_180_seed.explanation: Routine pediatric checkups occur at key intervals. The pediatrician measures weight, length, head circumference, assesses developmental milestones (sitting, tracking), and administers immunizations to protect against childhood diseases.

pathways.title: Routine Baby Visit
pathways.description: Comprehensive wellness check and immunization.
pathways.benefits: Early detection of developmental issues; vaccination defense.
pathways.risks: Mild vaccine fever or soreness.
pathways.considerations: Standard preventative care schedule.

questions: Is my baby meeting all developmental milestones for 6 months?
What side effects should I watch for after today's vaccinations?
When is the next scheduled wellness visit?

rep_181_seed.summary: Your primary care provider has assessed your blood pressure and diabetes management, modifying your prescriptions to improve control.
rep_181_seed.explanation: Managing high blood pressure and diabetes requires regular monitoring of vitals and blood chemistry. This routine visit ensures your medications are functioning properly without side effects, preventing long-term cardiovascular or renal damage.

pathways.title: Prescription Adjustment
pathways.description: Optimizing drug therapy with diet counsel.
pathways.benefits: Maintains BP < 130/80 and HbA1c < 7.0; prevents strokes and kidney damage.
pathways.risks: Potential drug interactions or minor side effects (cough, stomach upset).
pathways.considerations: Essential for active chronic disease control.

rep_182_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_182_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_182_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_187_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_187_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_187_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_192_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_192_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_192_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_205_seed.explanation: Routine pediatric checkups occur at key intervals. The pediatrician measures weight, length, head circumference, assesses developmental milestones (sitting, tracking), and administers immunizations to protect against childhood diseases.

pathways.description: Comprehensive wellness check and immunization.
pathways.benefits: Early detection of developmental issues; vaccination defense.

rep_206_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_206_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?
rep_206_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_207_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN has mapped out a vaginal childbirth plan, which is standard for healthy, uncomplicated term pregnancies.
rep_207_seed.explanation: Vaginal delivery is the natural birth process. The baby passes through the birth canal. It is the safest method of birth for both mother and baby for uncomplicated pregnancies, with minimal post-birth pain and hospital stay.

pathways.title: Normal Delivery
pathways.description: Vaginal birth with pain management options.
pathways.benefits: Shortest hospital stay; minimal surgical risks.
pathways.risks: Labor pains; potential minor tears.
pathways.considerations: Recommended for healthy pregnancies with vertex (head-down) baby.

questions: What pain management options (epidural, gas) will be available?
What triggers an emergency C-section during active labor?
How long will I remain in the facility post-birth?

rep_208_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_208_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_208_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_210_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_210_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_210_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_211_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.

pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

rep_212_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_212_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_212_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.

rep_231_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN has mapped out a vaginal childbirth plan, which is standard for healthy, uncomplicated term pregnancies.
rep_231_seed.explanation: Vaginal delivery is the natural birth process. The baby passes through the birth canal. It is the safest method of birth for both mother and baby for uncomplicated pregnancies, with minimal post-birth pain and hospital stay.

pathways.title: Normal Delivery
pathways.description: Vaginal birth with pain management options.
pathways.benefits: Shortest hospital stay; minimal surgical risks.

pathways.considerations: Recommended for healthy pregnancies with vertex (head-down) baby.

rep_232_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_232_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_232_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_238_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_238_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

questions: What are the risks if I choose to wait and manage with diet?
How will my digestion change after gallbladder removal?
Is this procedure done as day surgery, or will I be admitted overnight?

rep_240_seed.summary: Your surgical team recommends an emergency appendectomy to remove an inflamed appendix before it ruptures.
rep_240_seed.explanation: Appendicitis is a sudden swelling of the appendix. If not removed quickly, it can burst, spreading bacteria throughout the abdomen and causing life-threatening complications. The surgery removes the appendix via laparoscopy or open incision.

pathways.title: Emergency Appendectomy
pathways.description: Urgent removal of appendix.
pathways.benefits: Prevents rupture; immediate relief from severe infection.
pathways.risks: Infection of incision; brief recovery downtime.
pathways.considerations: Required standard of care for acute appendicitis.

questions: Is the appendix ruptured, or is it intact?
Will this be performed laparoscopically (keyhole) or via open surgery?
How long must I restrict heavy lifting or physical activity?

rep_245_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_245_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?
rep_245_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_260_seed.summary: Your surgeon recommends removing your gallbladder (cholecystectomy) using keyhole surgery to treat painful gallstone attacks.
rep_260_seed.explanation: The gallbladder stores bile. When gallstones form, they block bile ducts, causing severe pain after meals. Removing the gallbladder is the standard treatment. The liver will continue to produce bile, which will flow directly into your intestine instead.

pathways.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
pathways.description: Keyhole removal of the gallbladder.
pathways.benefits: Permanent cure; low pain; quick return to daily activities (1-2 weeks).
pathways.risks: Bile duct injury (rare); temporary digestive changes.
pathways.considerations: Surgical standard for recurring gallbladder pain.

pathways.title: Conservative Dietary Management
pathways.description: Avoiding fatty meals to prevent gallstone attacks.
pathways.benefits: Avoids surgery entirely.
pathways.risks: High risk of future attacks; potential infection or blockages (acute cholecystitis).
pathways.considerations: Only recommended for patients unable to undergo surgery due to health risks.

rep_261_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_261_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
Can we try 6 weeks of structured physical therapy before committing to surgery?
How long will I need crutches after the procedure?
rep_261_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_262_seed.summary: Your OB/GYN recommends a scheduled Cesarean Section (C-Section) delivery due to fetal presentation or maternal medical indications.
rep_262_seed.explanation: A Cesarean section is the surgical delivery of a baby through incisions in your abdomen and uterus. This is recommended when vaginal delivery poses risks, such as when the baby is in a breech (feet-first) position, or if there is severe fetal distress or placenta complications.

pathways.title: Cesarean Section (C-Section)
pathways.description: Surgical delivery via abdominal incision.
pathways.benefits: Ensures safe delivery in breech/compromised positions; avoids labor pain.
pathways.risks: Longer recovery time (6-8 weeks); risk of surgical infection, bleeding.
pathways.considerations: Clinically indicated for fetal breech or placenta previa.

pathways.title: Normal Vaginal Delivery
pathways.description: Natural vaginal delivery when labor progresses.
pathways.benefits: Quick recovery (1-2 weeks); lower risk of surgical complications.
pathways.risks: Risk of perineal tearing; unexpected emergency C-Section if labor fails.
pathways.considerations: Standard gold standard if baby rotates and no maternal complications exist.

questions: Why is a C-Section recommended instead of waiting for natural labor?
Are there any methods to rotate the baby (like external cephalic version) before scheduling?
What is the recovery protocol and does Cimas cover the cost difference ($1700 vs $185)?

rep_270_seed.summary: Your cardiologist recommends Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) surgery to route blood around blockages in three of your main heart arteries.
rep_270_seed.explanation: CABG (pronounced "cabbage") is open-heart surgery. Grafts (healthy blood vessels taken from your leg, chest, or arm) are used to bypass blocked arteries, restoring rich blood flow to your heart muscle, alleviating chest pain, and preventing heart attacks.

pathways.title: Coronary Artery Bypass (CABG)
pathways.description: Surgical bypass grafting around heart blockages.
pathways.benefits: Most complete revascularization; outstanding long-term survival in multi-vessel disease.
pathways.risks: Major surgery risks (bleeding, infection, stroke); long recovery (6-12 weeks).
pathways.considerations: Standard of care for severe three-vessel disease.

pathways.title: Angioplasty & Stenting (PCI)
pathways.description: Opening arteries with balloons and stents.
pathways.benefits: Minimally invasive; short hospital stay (1 day); quick recovery.
pathways.risks: Higher rate of blockages reoccurring; less effective for complex multi-vessel blockages.
pathways.considerations: Alternative for high-surgical-risk patients.

questions: Why is open-heart surgery preferred over stents for my blockages?
What is the expected length of stay in the cardiac ICU?
What cardiac rehab options are covered by Cimas?
rep_270_seed.disclaimer: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional.

rep_287_seed.summary: Your orthopedist recommends a knee arthroscopy (minimally invasive keyhole joint surgery) to repair or trim a torn meniscus.
rep_287_seed.explanation: The meniscus is a rubbery disc of cartilage that cushions your knee. A tear causes pain, swelling, and physical locking where the knee catches. Keyhole surgery is performed to insert a tiny camera and instruments to either sew the tear or trim away damaged flaps.

pathways.title: Arthroscopic Knee Surgery
pathways.description: Keyhole repair or trimming of the tear.
pathways.benefits: Resolves catching and locking; quick return to walking.
pathways.risks: Small risk of infection; does not fully eliminate future arthritis risks.
pathways.considerations: Indicated for unstable tears causing locking.

pathways.title: Physical Therapy & Rehabilitation
pathways.description: Conservative strengthening exercises.
pathways.benefits: No surgical risks; preserves all native cartilage.
pathways.risks: Locking and swelling may persist if the tear is large.
pathways.considerations: Reasonable first step for stable, non-locking tears.

questions: Is my tear in a zone that can be repaired (sewn) or must it be trimmed (removed)?
